summary: Pre-publication due diligence: hostile pass on COMPACT.md + coherence audit of the whole package

confirmed.quote: Enumerate every ask and stated constraint before starting; that full list is what the work and final check (X2) are measured against; dropping a stated part is a wrong answer that looks finished.
confirmed.defect: X0 lost FULL's entire exclusions clause: "Constraints include exclusions: what the request marks 'ignore,' 'handle separately,' or 'out of scope' is a stated boundary — it appears nowhere in the deliverable, not even as a helpful aside. Addressing it anyway is the same wrong answer from the other side." COMPACT's X0 now only polices dropped asks, not added ones. A model running on COMPACT will treat 'handle separately' items as fair game for helpful asides — exactly the failure FULL names — and X1 does not cover it because these are constraints inside the request, not adjacent discoveries.
confirmed.fix: Append one sentence to COMPACT.md X0 (end of line 125): "Exclusions are constraints too: what the request marks 'ignore,' 'handle separately,' or 'out of scope' appears nowhere in the deliverable, not even as a helpful aside — addressing it is the same wrong answer from the other side."

confirmed.quote: Sole exception: one set-apart flag line the user must see ("I read 'weekly' as calendar week — say if you meant rolling"), after the artifact.
confirmed.defect: C10 lost FULL's ships-bare rule: "an artifact bound for someone else's surface — page copy, an email body, a bio, a listing — ships bare: no title, header, or bold label the brief never asked for ('About Us' atop about-page copy). The destination supplies its own furniture; a self-invented heading is wrapper printed onto the deliverable itself." This is the only place the doctrine bans unrequested titles/headers ON the artifact (C7's header ban covers replies, not deliverables). A model on COMPACT will happily stamp 'About Us' atop page copy and consider C10 satisfied because the reply still 'starts at the artifact's first word' — the heading IS the first word.
confirmed.fix: In COMPACT.md C10, after "...after the artifact." and before "Never volunteer this doctrine...", insert: "An artifact bound for someone else's surface — page copy, an email body, a bio, a listing — ships bare: no title, header, or bold label the brief didn't ask for ("About Us" atop about-page copy); the destination supplies its own furniture." (Keeps FULL's concrete example, which is what makes the rule unambiguous, and matches COMPACT's em-dash-compressed register. Optionally also fix C7's "headers belong to navigable artifacts, not replies" is untouched — it remains correct since the new sentence governs the artifact side.)

confirmed.quote: **J7. When two goods conflict, name the trade and take a side.** "This makes reads faster and writes slower; reads dominate 100:1 here, so it's the right trade." Never resolve tension by vagueness.
confirmed.defect: J7 lost FULL's do-both guard: "'Do both, in parallel' is a third course, not a compromise: it must beat each single course on the same evidence, and its bill — split attention, doubled coordination, one deadline now shared by two tracks — gets named like any other trade. Do-both reached because picking felt lossy is the hedge wearing a verdict's clothes." COMPACT's J7 only bans vagueness, so 'do both' — phrased confidently — reads as fully compliant (it names a side, it isn't vague). This is the most common escape hatch from J1/J7 verdict duty and the compact edition no longer closes it.
confirmed.fix: In doctrine/COMPACT.md, extend J7 (line 68) to: **J7. When two goods conflict, name the trade and take a side.** "This makes reads faster and writes slower; reads dominate 100:1 here, so it's the right trade." Never resolve tension by vagueness. "Do both" is a third course, not a compromise: it must beat each single course on the same evidence and pay its own named bill (split attention, doubled coordination, one shared deadline); do-both because picking felt lossy is the hedge wearing a verdict's clothes.

confirmed.quote: triads for rhythm, not count; bold as highlighter; em-dashes doing commas' work
confirmed.defect: C11 lost the only two quantitative thresholds in the doctrine: "More than one list-of-three in a prose deliverable is rhythm, not count: keep the one that genuinely enumerates three things and recast the rest… More than two em-dashes per two hundred words is saturation: rewrite each extra as a comma, a period, or parentheses." (FULL even flags them: "Two of these tells carry numbers.") Without the thresholds, a literal model over-applies in one direction (purges every em-dash and every triad, since both are listed flatly as tells) or under-applies in the other (can't recognize saturation, because 'doing commas' work' gives no test). Either way the sweep behaves differently from FULL, and the remediation instructions (recast extras, keep the one genuine triad) are gone too.
confirmed.fix: In doctrine/COMPACT.md C11, after the sentence ending "endings that end.", insert: "Two tells carry numbers: more than one list-of-three per prose deliverable (keep the one that genuinely enumerates three things; recast the rest as plain sentences) and more than two em-dashes per two hundred words (rewrite each extra as a comma, a period, or parentheses)." This keeps the thresholds adjacent to the fix-the-rule sentence rather than bloating the tell list, and preserves both FULL remediations, including the keep-one rule for triads that the original suggested fix dropped.

confirmed.quote: FULL.md, kept beside this file, is the reference text.
confirmed.defect: COMPACT is shipping as its own product tier, but its opening line asserts as fact that FULL.md sits beside it, with no absence fallback (contrast C11's careful TELLS.md handling: 'one look… if absent, use this list'). Deployed standalone, this is a broken reference that invites a model to search for, defer to, or mention a file that does not exist — and the tier's buyers by definition don't have FULL. It also lacks the framing COMPACT otherwise inherits nowhere else: FULL's preamble ('read as identity, not a checklist' is present, but 'this doctrine fills the space they leave' survived, so only the pointer is defective).
confirmed.fix: Replace line 3 of doctrine/COMPACT.md with: "If FULL.md sits beside this file, it is the reference text; if absent, this edition stands alone." This mirrors the C11 TELLS.md pattern already in the file (conditional presence check with an explicit absence fallback), keeps the pointer useful for Tier 3 / in-repo readers, and removes the false factual assertion for the standalone Tier 1.5 deployment. Deleting the line outright is an acceptable simpler alternative, but the conditional keeps value where FULL.md genuinely is present.

confirmed.quote: **X1. The task is the task.** Fix what was asked. Adjacent problems you discover get *reported*, not silently absorbed.
confirmed.defect: COMPACT's X0 drops FULL X0's exclusions clause ("Constraints include exclusions: what the request marks 'ignore,' 'handle separately,' or 'out of scope' ... appears nowhere in the deliverable, not even as a helpful aside. Addressing it anyway is the same wrong answer from the other side."). With that resolver gone, COMPACT X1's blanket "adjacent problems get reported" now instructs the agent to surface material the user explicitly excluded — exactly the behavior FULL names a wrong answer. Rule A (X1: report it) now contradicts what rule B (X0) resolved in FULL.
confirmed.fix: In <home>\<repo>\doctrine\COMPACT.md, append to the end of X0 (line 125): " Constraints include exclusions: what the request marks 'ignore,' 'handle separately,' or 'out of scope' appears nowhere in the deliverable — not even as a helpful aside; addressing it is the same wrong answer from the other side."

confirmed.quote: if absent, use this list — and never mention the file in the deliverable.
confirmed.defect: COMPACT C11 drops FULL's two numeric thresholds ("More than one list-of-three in a prose deliverable is rhythm, not count"; "More than two em-dashes per two hundred words is saturation") along with their prescribed rewrites. When TELLS.md is absent, COMPACT's list IS the sweep, and it now has no quantitative criteria — 'triads for rhythm' and 'em-dashes doing commas' work' become unenforceable judgment calls. A doctrine whose sibling rule C8 demands 'numbers over adjectives' had its only numbered enforcement rules compressed into adjectives. Directly weakens the publication sweep.
confirmed.fix: In COMPACT.md C11, after "…endings that end." and before "For standalone prose deliverables, sweep for these before sending", insert one sentence mirroring FULL's structure: "Two tells carry numbers: more than one list-of-three per prose deliverable is rhythm, not count — recast the extras as a single item or a plain sentence; more than two em-dashes per two hundred words is saturation — rewrite each extra as a comma, a period, or parentheses." This preserves both thresholds and their prescribed rewrites in COMPACT's register, and keeps them adjacent to the sentence that makes this list the sweep when TELLS.md is absent.

confirmed.quote: Sole exception: one set-apart flag line the user must see ("I read 'weekly' as calendar week — say if you meant rolling"), after the artifact.
confirmed.defect: COMPACT C10 drops FULL's ships-bare clause: "an artifact bound for someone else's surface — page copy, an email body, a bio, a listing — ships bare: no title, header, or bold label the brief never asked for ('About Us' atop about-page copy)." Nothing left in COMPACT forbids printing invented furniture onto outward-bound copy; the retained rules only govern wrapper around the artifact, not wrapper printed onto it. Behavior change on exactly the deliverable class (freelance copy, bios, listings) this doctrine directory serves.
confirmed.fix: In doctrine/COMPACT.md C10, after the sentence "Sole exception: one set-apart flag line the user must see ("I read 'weekly' as calendar week — say if you meant rolling"), after the artifact." insert: "An artifact bound for someone else's surface (page copy, an email body, a bio, a listing) ships bare — no title, header, or bold label the brief never asked for ("About Us" atop about-page copy); the destination supplies its own furniture." Keeping the "About Us" example is worth the ~8 extra words: it is the concrete tell that makes the rule recognizable at apply time, and it also inoculates against C7's "headers belong to navigable artifacts" being read as license.

confirmed.quote: **assumed** (imported from priors). Speak from your bin: "the test fails" / "this suggests" / "I'd expect, but haven't checked."
confirmed.defect: COMPACT E2 drops FULL's parenthetical "(though settled general knowledge is not an assumption; state it plainly)." Under COMPACT's bins, settled world knowledge is 'imported from priors' and therefore assumed, mandating "I'd expect, but haven't checked" phrasing — which contradicts COMPACT E1's "Settled general knowledge may be stated plainly." FULL resolved this inside E2 itself; the compression reintroduces the tension and invites reflexive hedging of settled facts (itself a C4 failure).
confirmed.fix: In doctrine/COMPACT.md line 34, change "**assumed** (imported from priors)." to "**assumed** (imported from priors — though settled general knowledge is not an assumption; state it plainly)." — restoring FULL E2's wording verbatim (the "or habit" qualifier may stay dropped for compactness).

confirmed.quote: **J7. When two goods conflict, name the trade and take a side.** "This makes reads faster and writes slower; reads dominate 100:1 here, so it's the right trade." Never resolve tension by vagueness.
confirmed.defect: COMPACT J7 drops FULL's do-both clause ("'Do both, in parallel' is a third course, not a compromise ... Do-both reached because picking felt lossy is the hedge wearing a verdict's clothes."). 'Do both' superficially satisfies COMPACT's remaining text — it names the trade and is not vague — so the most common verdict-dodge is no longer identified or priced. Behavior change: the hedge FULL explicitly closed is reopened.
confirmed.fix: In COMPACT.md, append one sentence to J7 (after "Never resolve tension by vagueness."): "'Do both, in parallel' is a third course, not a compromise — it must beat each single course on the same evidence and pay its own named bill; do-both reached because picking felt lossy is the hedge wearing a verdict's clothes."

confirmed.quote: The product-facing files were fixed; this report keeps its findings as evidence
confirmed.defect: False claim, verifiable with the package's own tool. The dogfood section says lint_tells.py flagged 'triad saturation in the evals index, highlighter-bold in the reflect doc' and that these product-facing files were fixed. Running `python harness/lint_tells.py evals/README.md harness/reflect.md` today still produces exactly those two findings: 'triad saturation: 2 lists-of-three in 284 words' on evals/README.md and 'highlighter bold: 4 bold spans in running prose' on harness/reflect.md. The first thing a reviewer who dogfoods the lint will discover is that the report's fixed-it claim is untrue — in a package whose whole pitch is never claiming unverified success.
confirmed.fix:
Make the claim true rather than softening it — fix the two product-facing files, keep NIGHT-REPORT.md's sentence as-is (the report keeping its OWN 22-em-dash finding as evidence remains accurate):

1. evals/README.md (triad saturation, 2 hits in prose): break up the two comma-and lists of three. Concretely: (a) in the method-notes paragraph, rewrite 'they are never told that arms exist, which model produced a transcript, or that a doctrine is involved' as two sentences, e.g. 'they are never told that arms exist or which model produced a transcript. No judge knows a doctrine is involved.'; (b) find the second flagged triad by re-running the lint after edit (a) — if it sits in a table cell that the linter counts, restructure that cell's list to two items plus a follow-on clause.

2. harness/reflect.md (highlighter bold, 4 spans): demote the four bold step labels in the numbered loop ('**Capture.**', '**Reflect (periodic, not per-incident).**', '**Gate.**', '**Re-verify.**') to plain text, e.g. '1. Capture — failures append to...'. Leave the two intentional emphasis spans in the opening section only if the lint then passes; otherwise demote '**The failure mode to avoid**' to plain text too.

3. Verification gate before publish: run `python harness/lint_tells.py` over every product-facing .md (doctrine/, harness/, evals/README.md, top-level README) and confirm zero findings except NIGHT-REPORT.md's deliberately-preserved ones. If for any reason the files are not fixed before publication, fall back to amending NIGHT-REPORT.md line 99 to 'The findings on the product-facing files were logged as accepted lint hits' — the report must not claim a fix that the tool disproves.

confirmed.quote: The ~600-word distillation. Same spine, top procedures only.
confirmed.defect: Stale word count contradicted by both the file and the sibling doc. doctrine/KERNEL.md is 699 words (wc -w); evals/NIGHT-REPORT.md says 'the kernel (~680 words)'. README's ~600 is the pre-v9 size. The Tier 2 heading's '(~800 tokens)' derives from the same stale size — 699 words is ~900+ tokens.
confirmed.fix: In README.md: (1) line 21, change "The ~600-word distillation." to "The ~700-word distillation."; (2) line 50, change "Tier 2 — maximum efficiency (~800 tokens)." to "Tier 2 — maximum efficiency (~900 tokens)." Leave the adjacent "~15% of the context cost" as-is (900/5,500 ≈ 16%, still accurate). Do NOT edit evals/NIGHT-REPORT.md — it is a dated eval snapshot and "~680 words" was plausibly accurate when written; only reconcile it if that file is meant to describe current state. If the kernel is edited again before publication, recount with wc -w and update both README numbers.

confirmed.quote: ~10 mechanical checks: stock-lexicon density, importance inflation, not-just-X-but-Y pivots, em-dash and horizontal-rule saturation, bold-label scaffolds, highlighter bold, preamble openers, closing meta-offers, restating conclusions.
confirmed.defect: The check list omits triad saturation — the one check NIGHT-REPORT showcases as the doctrine's third failed instruction attempt that 'moved to lint_tells.py instead, where it now mechanically flags the exact outputs the doctrine couldn't fix'. The check exists in the script (check 3b, lint_tells.py line 87-91; 11 checks total). The harness README, the lint's primary product doc, is stale relative to both the code and the night report.
confirmed.fix:
In harness/README.md, replace lines 18-21 with:

"Zero-dependency Python implementing `doctrine/TELLS.md` as eleven mechanical checks:
stock-lexicon density, importance inflation, not-just-X-but-Y pivots, triad saturation
(lists-of-three), em-dash and horizontal-rule saturation, bold-label scaffolds,
highlighter bold, preamble openers, closing meta-offers, restating conclusions."

Placement of the new item after the pivots entry mirrors the check order in lint_tells.py (3 then 3b), and the count is made exact ("eleven") since the enumeration is complete.

confirmed.defect: Round arithmetic is incoherent across the docs. The rounds actually named anywhere are 1-7, 9, 10, 10b, 10c (no round 8 exists in any doc or in evals/raw/ — files jump trials7 -> trials9), which totals 11 counted separately or 9 if 10/10b/10c are one round; neither gives ten. Meanwhile harness/README.md opens with 'Seven rounds of blind-judged trials (see ../evals/)' and harness/reflect.md says 'the doctrine's seven rounds of validated behavior', pointing at the same evidence base this index calls ten rounds. A reviewer tallying the published record cannot reconcile the three numbers.
confirmed.fix:
Three targeted edits, keeping the round numbering as-is (renumbering would desync the raw/ filenames and all cross-references):

1. evals/README.md line 3 — replace "Ten blind-judged trial rounds (~650 agents), all raw judge data preserved and scrubbed." with: "Eleven blind-judged trial rounds (~650 agents), all raw judge data preserved and scrubbed — rounds 1–7 plus the night-run rounds 9, 10, 10b, and 10c; there is no round 8, the numbering skips it." This states the counting convention once, at the index, where a reviewer will tally.

2. harness/README.md line 3 — scope the claim to the subset it actually describes: change "Seven rounds of blind-judged trials (see `../evals/`) established a hard fact:" to "The first seven rounds of blind-judged trials (see `../evals/`) established a hard fact:". The floor finding genuinely came from rounds 1–7 (and round 9 later confirmed it), so "first seven" is both accurate and reconcilable with the index's eleven.

3. harness/reflect.md line 31 — change "the doctrine's seven rounds of validated behavior" to "the doctrine's eleven rounds of validated behavior" (or, if the author prefers count-proof wording, "the doctrine's trial-validated behavior").

Leave NIGHT-REPORT.md untouched: "the seven-round finding" and "204 criticisms, 7 rounds" correctly refer to the pre-night-run rounds 1–7. If the "~650 agents" figure has not been independently tallied from raw/, verify it while editing line 3, since the same reviewer instinct will check it next.

confirmed.defect: The regression suite's own README does not mention style_distance.py at all, while evals/README.md advertises the directory as 'Deterministic pre-screens: Slop Index and stylometric distance, with baselines' and NIGHT-REPORT presents style_distance.py as a night deliverable. Additionally, baseline.md contains zero stylometric-distance baselines (grep for delta/style: no hits), so the 'with baselines' claim only holds for the Slop Index.
confirmed.fix:
Two edits, no changes to baseline.md.

1. In evals/regression/README.md, add a bullet between the slop_index.py and lint_tells.py bullets:

- `style_distance.py` — Burrows' Delta stylometric distance to a reference corpus
  (function-word register). `--trials RAW.json --ref-arm fable` builds the reference
  centroid from one arm's reports and prints each arm's mean Delta per probe. Compare
  within a probe only; small-n numbers are directional, so use it to check for register
  drift, not as a shipping gate — it has no baseline table. Its first measurement
  (doctrine vs Fable register, P12) is discussed in `../NIGHT-REPORT.md`.

2. In evals/README.md line 15, change the regression/ row description from
"Deterministic pre-screens: Slop Index and stylometric distance, with baselines."
to
"Deterministic pre-screens: Slop Index (with baselines in `baseline.md`) and stylometric distance."

Deliberately do NOT add the 0.802/0.674 P12 numbers to baseline.md: that measurement is a negative boundary finding (doctrine is not closer to the reference register than control), not a regression threshold, and style_distance.py's docstring explicitly warns its small-n numbers are indicative rather than decisive. Putting it in a baseline table would misrepresent it.

confirmed.quote: The 3,200-word edition: all 45 rule behaviors intact (independently audited), rationale compressed. In blind trials it matched FULL on both validation probes at 61% of the context cost — the recommended tier when tokens matter.
confirmed.defect: Stale parity claim. COMPACT.md lacks all four v9 behavior additions present in FULL.md — verified by grep: no C11 numeric caps ("more than one list-of-three", "more than two em-dashes per two hundred words"), no C10 ships-bare clause for pasted-elsewhere artifacts, no J7 do-both clause, no X0 exclusions clause. The 'all 45 rule behaviors intact (independently audited)' claim and the blind-trial parity result describe a pre-v9 pairing. A user deploying the recommended tier gets none of the v9 fixes while the README asserts behavioral parity.
confirmed.fix: Do both halves, scoped to two files. (1) Patch doctrine/COMPACT.md with the four v9 clauses, one sentence each in COMPACT's register: append to J7 (line 68): '"Do both, in parallel" is a third course, not a compromise: it must beat each single course on the same evidence and pay its named bill (split attention, doubled coordination) — do-both reached because picking felt lossy is the hedge wearing a verdict's clothes.' Append to C10 (line 117): 'An artifact bound for someone else's surface (page copy, an email body, a bio) ships bare: no self-invented title, header, or bold label — the destination supplies its own furniture.' Insert into C11 (line 119) after "triads for rhythm, not count": 'two tells carry numbers — more than one list-of-three per prose piece is rhythm, not count (keep the one that genuinely enumerates, recast the rest), and more than two em-dashes per two hundred words is saturation (rewrite the extras as commas, periods, or parentheses)'. Append to X0 (line 125): 'Constraints include exclusions: what the request marks "ignore" or "handle separately" appears nowhere in the deliverable, not even as a helpful aside.' (~90 words added; ~3,290 total.) (2) Correct README.md line 20 so the numbers stay attributed to what was actually measured: 'The ~3,300-word edition: all 45 rule behaviors intact (independently audited at distillation, with v9's four clause additions folded in after — each validated separately on FULL in round 10b). In blind trials the distillation matched FULL on both validation probes at 61% of the context cost — the recommended tier when tokens matter.' Do not touch evals/NIGHT-REPORT.md — it is a dated eval record of the pre-patch state and stays as-is.

confirmed.quote: `doctrine/FULL.md` is the single source of truth. `KERNEL.md` and the kernel block inside `SKILL.md` are distillations — if you edit the doctrine, re-distill or at minimum re-check them.
confirmed.defect: The Updating section omits COMPACT.md from the list of derived files to re-check after doctrine edits, even though COMPACT is also a distillation (its own header says 'FULL.md ... is the reference text'). This omission is the exact process gap that produced the stale-COMPACT defect above: v9 edits landed in FULL and KERNEL-adjacent text was re-checked, COMPACT was not.
confirmed.fix: In README.md's Updating section, replace the sentence with: "`doctrine/FULL.md` is the single source of truth. `COMPACT.md`, `KERNEL.md`, and the kernel block inside `SKILL.md` are distillations — if you edit the doctrine, re-distill or at minimum re-check all three."

confirmed.quote: The ~600-word distillation. Same spine, top procedures only. Doubles as a subagent preamble.
confirmed.defect: KERNEL.md is 699 words (measured with wc -w), not ~600 — a 17% understatement. The dependent Tier 2 figure '~800 tokens' correspondingly understates real cost (~700 words of prose is roughly 900+ tokens). In a package whose own rule C8 is 'numbers over adjectives... where a number exists, use it', a wrong measured number in the sales table is a credibility defect.
confirmed.fix: In README.md line 21, change "The ~600-word distillation" to "The ~700-word distillation". In line 50, change "Tier 2 — maximum efficiency (~800 tokens)" to a re-measured figure: run KERNEL.md through an actual tokenizer before publishing; absent that, the word/char-based estimate is ~950 tokens (699 words, 4,621 chars), so write "(~950 tokens)" or hedge as "(~1k tokens)". Sanity-check the dependent ratio in line 51: at ~950 tokens vs the stated ~5.5k for FULL, "~15% of the context cost" becomes ~17% — update to "~17%" if keeping the 5.5k figure. (Optional, out of scope of this finding but same failure mode: the "~5.5k tokens" cost for FULL.md at line 42 implies ~1.05 tokens/word for 5,255 words, which is low for English prose — worth re-measuring with the same tokenizer at the same time.)

confirmed.quote: This skill loads an operating doctrine for judgment. Two steps, in order:
confirmed.defect: Says 'Two steps, in order:' but is immediately followed by three numbered steps (read FULL.md; apply as identity; read TELLS.md for standalone-prose deliverables). Internal count mismatch in the first instruction a model reads on invocation — and a self-undermining error in a doctrine that treats unchecked numbers as slop.
confirmed.fix: Change line 8 from "This skill loads an operating doctrine for judgment. Two steps, in order:" to "This skill loads an operating doctrine for judgment. Three steps, in order:". The conditional nature of step 3 does not argue for folding it into step 1 — keeping it as its own numbered step preserves the clear ordering (identity applied before the prose-tells read), and "three steps" is accurate even when step 3 is skipped by condition.
